purpose: Secret-free index of narrow live/local evidence that can be rendered without upgrading unverified protocol claims.

allowed_claims: Radicle local CLI verified for one disposable private temporary-RAD_HOME init/inspect replay
Nostr selected-relay acceptance/readback verified for one exact prototype event on two relays
Radicle disposable public seed/remote-clone smoke verified for exact RID rad:z2WtozFrCRhygh9CGzyUN57CN7Nwa
GitHub Actions generated keyless hosted artifact attestations for six prototype artifacts on main
Helia local UnixFS/IPFS add-get verified for the local release artifact fixture bytes
Public gateway preflight queried three gateways and observed no successful readback for the unpinned fixture CID
Nostr selected-relay issue/patch acceptance/readback verified for two disposable prototype events
Radicle broader check recorded current-host rad CLI absence plus read-only public route probes
Registry-shaped keyless attestation import recorded outside project-registry fixtures
Current decentralized-forge commit initialized as a public Radicle repo and cloned/read back from a separate temporary profile
Recorded Radicle RID cloned/read back from a fresh temporary Radicle profile without reusing the original Loop 59 seed profile
Current project commit published to the recorded Radicle RID under a fresh peer namespace, while default readback remained on the original delegate commit
Retained project-scoped Radicle maintainer state published a new RID and fresh default plus direct-seed readbacks matched the source commit
The same retained Radicle RID advanced from the Loop 62 commit to the next Git commit, with fresh direct-seed readback matching the updated commit
The same retained Radicle RID advanced to the current published commit, a fresh reader cloned from the retained seed, and a second fresh reader cloned from that first reader acting as a follower seed

The same retained Radicle RID advanced to the current published commit, a fresh reader cloned from the retained seed before restart, and another fresh reader cloned from the retained seed after restart on the same local address
A public VPS follower seed served the retained Radicle RID to a fresh reader from a separate Linux machine, and readback matched the expected commit
The public openclaw VPS follower seed runs as an enabled user-level systemd service and served the retained RID after an explicit service restart
A repeatable public Radicle seed health-check script cloned the retained RID from the explicit openclaw seed and verified the expected commit
The retained RID advanced to commit 64efbada294d4a57c014a27398b92e344c6d68aa and the public openclaw follower seed served that updated commit to a fresh reader
An external ubuntu-work user systemd timer ran the public Radicle seed health check and wrote a passing latest.json for the openclaw seed
The retained RID advanced to commit ef16e2ad39d3e13bdcc9d454443c5bbb17733c68 and the public openclaw follower seed served that updated commit to a fresh reader
A second persistent follower seed on ubuntu-work served the retained RID to a fresh reader on openclaw over Tailnet, with readback matching commit ef16e2ad39d3e13bdcc9d454443c5bbb17733c68
forbidden_claims: Radicle durable public seed/network availability verified
Nostr durability or global propagation verified
full NIP-34 or forge compatibility verified

evidence.verification_summary: Temporary RAD_HOME plus disposable Git repository completed rad auth, private no-seed rad init, and local rad inspect commands.
evidence.notes: Promotes only the disposable private local replay path. Later Loop 34 evidence separately records one bounded disposable public seed/clone smoke; this row does not imply public-network availability.

evidence.verification_summary: Exact prototype/research kind 30617 event was published to and read back from selected relays; local and readback nak verify returned exit code 0.
non_claims: not full NIP-34 or forge protocol compatibility
evidence.notes: Uses only the disposable project key; secret key material remains outside the repo and is not recorded here.

evidence.verification_summary: One disposable public Radicle repository was initialized, seeded from a temporary localhost node, synced/announced, cloned through a separate temporary node, and read back successfully.
non_claims: not proof of broad Radicle network availability
evidence.notes: Uses only disposable temporary state recorded in Loop 34 evidence. No production/private personal keys, paid infrastructure, direct outreach, persistent seed operation, or broad network availability claim.

evidence.verification_summary: GitHub Actions run 28339280081 generated a keyless hosted attestation with SLSA provenance predicate for six prototype artifacts after CI passed on main.
non_claims: not proof of production supply-chain security
not a registry fixture provenance import
not proof of consumer policy verification
evidence.notes: Uses GitHub OIDC/keyless attestation; no production/private personal signing key is recorded or used.

evidence.verification_summary: A project-scoped, non-started in-process Helia instance added fixtures/local-release-artifact.txt to local UnixFS/IPFS storage and read back the exact bytes for CID bafkreibzglri2w3atm6k4jjbrsral2qsntj46ncgfdoeys436ckmkbtiua.
non_claims: not proof of public gateway availability
evidence.notes: Uses only project-scoped npm dev dependencies and local fixture bytes. No paid storage, wallet, public gateway query, persistent daemon, or production/private personal key was used.

evidence.verification_summary: Three public gateways were queried for the local release artifact CID; all timed out and no gateway returned bytes matching the fixture. No pinning provider action was taken.
non_claims: not proof of public gateway availability
evidence.notes: Records one low-volume gateway observation and pinning preflight only. No pinning provider account/token, paid storage, wallet, daemon, or persistent service was used.

evidence.verification_summary: Two disposable NIP-34-shaped collaboration events, one issue and one patch, were published to and read back from selected relays with local and readback signature verification.
non_claims: not proof of full NIP-34 or forge protocol compatibility
evidence.notes: Uses one in-memory disposable key generated by nostr-tools. Secret key material is not recorded.

evidence.verification_summary: The current Windows host has no rad CLI available, so broader disposable CLI clone/sync was blocked; read-only public Radicle web/node route probes were recorded for the prior disposable RID.
non_claims: not a broader Radicle CLI verification
not proof of broad Radicle network availability
evidence.notes: No Radicle identity, RAD_HOME, node, seed, publish, sync, clone, connect, or remote command was run.

evidence.verification_summary: Hosted GitHub keyless attestation evidence was imported into a registry-shaped verification row outside the project-registry fixtures.
non_claims: not a replacement for fixtures/example-project.registry.json ci.provenance
not proof of production supply-chain security
not proof of consumer policy verification
evidence.notes: The imported row is registry-shaped evidence only; the existing registry fixture provenance row remains synthetic.

evidence.verification_summary: The current decentralized-forge commit was initialized as a public Radicle repository, seeded from disposable temporary state, cloned by a separate temporary Radicle profile, and read back at the exact source commit.

public_identifiers.seed_scope: temporary Radicle home and localhost seed node
public_identifiers.clone_scope: separate temporary Radicle home and clone node

non_claims: not proof of broad Radicle network availability
evidence.notes: Uses only temporary project-scoped Radicle state in a Linux container. Private key material and passphrase were not recorded, and temporary seed/clone state was removed after evidence capture.

evidence.verification_summary: The Loop 59 Radicle RID was cloned from a brand-new temporary Radicle profile without reusing the original seed profile or explicitly connecting to the original seed node, and the cloned commit matched the expected Loop 59 commit.
evidence.notes: Fresh-state readback was observed once through Radicle's normal network path. This strengthens the project-repo evidence beyond localhost-only smoke but is still not a long-term availability guarantee.

evidence.verification_summary: A fresh non-original Radicle identity pushed the current project commit to the same RID under its own peer namespace, but a default fresh clone still checked out the original delegate main at the prior commit.
non_claims: not proof of canonical default-branch update continuity
evidence.notes: The useful finding is architectural: same-RID non-original peer publication works, but default clone/readback follows the original delegate branch. A real usable decentralized repo needs retained maintainer delegate state or a deliberate multi-peer/ref policy.

evidence.verification_summary: A retained project-scoped Radicle maintainer state under gitignored .tmp published RID rad:z3Q8ePG6Qs4PQi1SWf9BEzDayENcy, and fresh default plus explicit-seed readbacks matched the source commit.
non_claims: not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a committed secret or key backup
evidence.notes: This is the first retained maintainer lane: the reusable Radicle identity/worktree remain outside Git under .tmp so future updates can target the same RID. It still does not keep a persistent public seed service running or prove long-term availability.

evidence.verification_summary: The retained Radicle RID advanced from the Loop 62 commit to commit f800bae387f33452fdeb79ecf5c795d25f7246ac; default public-routing readback was not observed, but fresh explicit direct-seed readback matched the updated commit.
non_claims: not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a committed secret or key backup
evidence.notes: This proves the retained maintainer lane can advance the same RID when the retained state is available. The observed default clone failed in this run, so the supported readback claim is explicit direct-seed readback.

evidence.verification_summary: The retained Radicle RID advanced to current commit 7262f69b82e442263d6261414f6b771be04c6b6f; reader A cloned from the retained maintainer seed and then served as a follower seed for reader B, whose clone read back the same commit.

public_identifiers.reader_state_scope: temporary WSL /tmp reader states removed after evidence capture

non_claims: not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a persistent public seed service
not a committed secret or key backup
evidence.notes: This is the first retained-RID follower-seed readback: one fresh reader cloned from the retained maintainer seed, then a second fresh reader cloned from the first reader acting as a follower seed. It improves usability evidence but still does not keep a seed service running or prove durable availability.

evidence.verification_summary: The retained Radicle RID advanced to current commit 4c8cd6183e2e12d1c62af7d2f013fb085b2d6bf8; a fresh reader cloned from the retained seed before restart, the seed stopped and restarted on the same local address with the same node ID, and a second fresh reader cloned the same commit after restart.

public_identifiers.reader_state_scope: temporary WSL /tmp reader states removed after evidence capture

non_claims: not proof of future default public-routing availability
not proof of public seed address reachability
not an always-on persistent public seed service
not a committed secret or key backup
evidence.notes: This is a retained-RID seed restart rehearsal: one fresh reader cloned before restart and another cloned after restart from the retained seed on the same local address. It improves operational usability evidence but still does not publish a reachable public seed address or prove durable availability.

evidence.verification_summary: The openclaw VPS follower seed at z6Mkk7qWfxE18R4jt5ruXmv1a7zydT9r1sY5LXx21PWySA4f@187.77.19.162:8776 served retained RID rad:z3Q8ePG6Qs4PQi1SWf9BEzDayENcy to a fresh reader on ubuntu-work, whose clone read back commit 610fc3da9757d0cb123aa5976db552b991b766d4.
non_claims: not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a committed secret or key backup
not maintainer key material on the VPS
evidence.notes: This is the first public direct-seed readback through a VPS follower seed. It proves a fresh reader can clone the retained RID from the published openclaw seed address for this run, while preserving the boundary that durability, default public routing, and production readiness are not claimed.

evidence.verification_summary: The openclaw VPS follower seed was installed as an enabled user-level systemd service with restart policy and linger enabled; after explicit service restart, a fresh ubuntu-work reader cloned retained RID rad:z3Q8ePG6Qs4PQi1SWf9BEzDayENcy at commit 610fc3da9757d0cb123aa5976db552b991b766d4.
non_claims: not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a committed secret or key backup
not maintainer key material on the VPS
evidence.notes: This hardens the public follower seed with user-level systemd and validates post-restart direct-seed readback, while preserving bounded non-claims.

evidence.verification_summary: scripts/check_public_radicle_seed.py created a fresh temporary reader profile on ubuntu-work, connected to the explicit openclaw seed, cloned the retained RID, and verified commit 610fc3da9757d0cb123aa5976db552b991b766d4.
non_claims: not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a committed secret or key backup
evidence.notes: This turns public seed verification into a repeatable command, but the result remains point-in-time evidence.

evidence.verification_summary: The retained RID advanced from 610fc3da9757d0cb123aa5976db552b991b766d4 to 64efbada294d4a57c014a27398b92e344c6d68aa; the openclaw follower synced through a temporary maintainer bridge, then served the updated commit to a fresh ubuntu-work reader after the bridge and maintainer seed were stopped.
non_claims: not proof of automatic future update propagation
not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a committed secret or key backup
not maintainer key material on the VPS
evidence.notes: This is the first proof that the public follower seed can serve a later retained-RID commit after manual maintainer-to-follower sync.

evidence.verification_summary: ubuntu-work runs an enabled user-level systemd timer that invokes scripts/check_public_radicle_seed.py against the openclaw public seed; the first forced run wrote a passing latest.json for commit ef16e2ad39d3e13bdcc9d454443c5bbb17733c68.
non_claims: not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a committed secret or key backup
evidence.notes: This adds external observability for the public seed but does not prove durability or automatic remediation.

evidence.verification_summary: The retained RID advanced from 64efbada294d4a57c014a27398b92e344c6d68aa to ef16e2ad39d3e13bdcc9d454443c5bbb17733c68; the openclaw follower synced it through a temporary maintainer bridge, then served the updated commit to a fresh ubuntu-work reader after the bridge and maintainer seed were stopped.
non_claims: not proof of automatic future update propagation
not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a committed secret or key backup
not maintainer key material on the VPS
evidence.notes: This updates the public direct-seed milestone to include the prior hardening commit and records that external health monitoring is now installed.

evidence.verification_summary: ubuntu-work runs a second persistent follower seed with separate Radicle state and node identity; a fresh reader on openclaw connected over Tailnet to z6MksRdjzuN2VYV4HTXdVSchitJ8Bq1zbx8WhBb3KhyfSm6A@100.83.206.66:8877, cloned the retained RID, and verified commit ef16e2ad39d3e13bdcc9d454443c5bbb17733c68.
non_claims: not a second public internet seed yet
not proof of future default public-routing availability
not a committed secret or key backup
not maintainer key material on ubuntu-work
evidence.notes: The openclaw-to-ubuntu-work Tailnet readback proves a second persistent follower seed and separate state store. Opening openclaw public port 8877 as an internet relay remains blocked until explicit approval because it changes the VPS public network surface.
